meta.title: Kevin Andrew — quiet systems for loud problems
meta.description: Kevin Andrew builds cloud infrastructure, protocols, and applied AI — designed from first principles and shipped end to end.

home.lede: I run on caffeine, playlists, and "what if?" Most days you'll find me designing, building, researching, or convincing myself that starting another side project is a good idea. If an idea keeps me awake at night, it's probably worth building.
home.workKeynote: If you're still here, you'll probably enjoy these too
home.workKeynoteBody: Just a few corners of the internet where I read, write, learn, and occasionally disappear for a few hours.
home.featuredSub: STEM — cloning production without the blast radius
home.manifesto: I don't really have a process. I notice something, get obsessed with it for a while, build until it works, write down what I learned, and then accidentally find the next thing to obsess over.
home.doorsWritingBody: Mostly notes to myself. Maybe they'll help you too.
home.doorsResearchBody: Some problems need more than code. Formal work on provenance and resource allocation — and the open questions behind it.
home.doorsStudioBody: A small practice for people who need systems that simply work. Select client work, and how it runs.
home.now1: Building Etch — cryptographic provenance for code. Open-core, spec written in public.
home.now2: A paper on cloud resource allocation is under double-blind review.
home.now3: Edith Studio is open for select client engagements.

work.title: Work
work.subtitle: Here are the things that kept me busy when I probably should've been sleeping.
work.transition: Turns out **"just one more project"** is never just one.
work.archiveNote: Links land here as each project's public artifact is verified — nothing on this site ships a dead end.

aboutPage.short: Hey again! It's me, Kevin. I'm just a friendly neighbourhood guy who loves chasing ideas. Most days begin with coffee, music, and the promise that I'll "only read one article." A few hours later, I'm knee-deep in research papers, design inspiration, random Wikipedia pages, or documentation that somehow sparked another idea worth building. I like looking at the world through both logic and creativity because, in my experience, the best ideas usually live somewhere in between.
aboutPage.howHeading: A curiosity that never sits still
aboutPage.how1: Building has always been my favourite way of learning.
aboutPage.how2: Over the years, that mindset has taken me from side projects to founding <studio>Edith Studio</studio>, where I design and build products for clients across different parts of the world. Every collaboration has reinforced the same lesson: great products aren't built by adding more—they're built by understanding what truly matters.
aboutPage.how3: See how Etch grew from a late-night question into a cryptographic protocol.
aboutPage.how4: Alongside client work, research has quietly become a second home. I enjoy investigating ideas that sit between software engineering, distributed systems, AI, and design, then turning them into something people can actually use, read, or build upon.
aboutPage.how5: When I'm not working with clients or writing papers, I'm probably chasing another idea that started with "I wonder if..."
aboutPage.stop1Body: Founded a design and development studio, helping ideas grow into thoughtful digital products for clients around the world.
aboutPage.stop2Body: Built modern fintech experiences, turning complex workflows into clean, intuitive interfaces with React and Tailwind CSS.
aboutPage.stop3Body: Explored AI and cloud technologies through hands-on projects, learning how intelligent systems move from prototypes to production.
aboutPage.stop4Body: Worked on applied AI and machine learning, experimenting with practical solutions to real-world problems.
aboutPage.el1: Deep space — the aesthetic and the physics, both.
aboutPage.el3: Theology and physics — different instruments, same question.

contactPage.title: Let's build something that lasts.
contactPage.body: For client work, research, collaboration, or a question worth asking — the fastest route is email. Replies usually within a day.
